stages.purpose: Convert human need into bounded paper commitments and owner decisions.

subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Owner semantics are controller-owned; a second lane is needed only when commitments change or freeze.

stages.completion_gate: confirmed owner decision or recorded owner-gated assumption; no worker may silently change this stage
stages.activation_policy: activate when owner motivation, venue, claim scope, or private-source policy is unclear

stages.purpose: Make raw paper inputs locatable and support-safe.

subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Inventory work is mostly structural but needs independent verification when source support or evidence admissibility is uncertain.

stages.completion_gate: claim-relevant sources are locator-backed or explicitly unresolved
stages.activation_policy: activate when source or evidence inventory is missing/stale

stages.purpose: Understand field scene, template expectations, exemplar structure, and related-work positioning.

subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Research/SOTA materials shape all downstream claims, so producer output must be independently checked before commit.

stages.completion_gate: research dossier and template/profile outputs are source-located and safe to consume
stages.activation_policy: activate when field/template/SOTA is uncertain

stages.purpose: Identify viable contribution framings without treating speculation as evidence.

subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Novelty framing is high-risk and speculative by nature; independent challenge prevents unsupported contribution claims.

stages.completion_gate: viable options are classified as supported, owner-gated, or rejected
stages.activation_policy: activate when contribution is unstable or weak

stages.purpose: Admit, weaken, reject, or backflow every claim-bearing unit against evidence anchors, support strength, allowed wording, forbidden wording, and result-package boundaries.

subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Claim-evidence admissibility is a safety-critical gate; production and verification must remain separate.

stages.completion_gate: every claim-bearing unit is admitted, weakened, rejected, owner-gated, or backflowed with support strength, evidence anchor, allowed wording, forbidden wording, result boundary, and downstream permission
stages.activation_policy: activate before claim-bearing writing or review

stages.purpose: Build a claim-bounded reader-question spine from S04-admitted claim capsules, with coverage ledgers and downstream S06/S07/S08 handoffs; no new claims or final prose.

subagent_lane_policy.rationale: The paper spine controls global argument order; independent challenge catches incoherent or unsupported reader paths.

stages.completion_gate: S05ReaderSpine validates every S04 intake claim, reader question, section placement, promise payoff, owner decision, and S06/S07/S08 handoff without new claims or final prose
stages.activation_policy: activate for new section or major rewrite

stages.purpose: Design complete object and mechanism-variable representation, granularity progression, section/load budgets, explanation ladders, and downstream handoffs without adding claims or drafting prose.

escalate_to_double_when: new object family or granularity ladder is introduced
subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Object/granularity design is reusable control material; single production is enough unless it changes multi-section semantics.

stages.completion_gate: S06ObjectGranularity validates object/variable coverage, cards, cross maps, granularity/load budgets, explanation ladders, repetition checks, unresolved objects, and S07/S08/S10 handoffs without new claims or final prose
stages.activation_policy: activate for full paper route or when object repetition risk exists

stages.purpose: Compile claim-safe rhetoric, terminology, paragraph jobs, flexible surface controls, forbidden expressions, coverage ledgers, and downstream handoffs without final prose or claim strengthening.

escalate_to_double_when: surface rules govern multiple writing units
subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Surface and terminology controls are broad but usually locally checkable; escalate when they constrain claims across sections.

stages.completion_gate: S07RhetoricSurfaceControl validates claim surface rules, terminology coverage, internal-id bans, paragraph jobs, flexible language controls, forbidden expressions, coverage ledger, and S09/S10/S12/S13 handoffs without new claims or final prose
stages.activation_policy: activate before writing execution

stages.purpose: Compile reader questions, section/object budgets, claim/evidence boundaries, and source-data routes into visual/formal object contracts.

subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Visual/formal planning is often design-led and evidence-linked; escalate before paper-facing visual commitments freeze.

stages.completion_gate: S08VisualFormalPlan validates visual needs, candidates, visual budget, figure/table/formal contracts, panel evidence maps, claim bindings, backend routes, caption boundaries, accessibility constraints, coverage ledger, and S10/S11/S12/S13 handoffs without final figures, final captions, new claims, or completion overclaim
stages.activation_policy: activate before any figure/table/algorithm/formula becomes paper-facing or when S05/S06/S04 imply a visual/formal object need

stages.purpose: Select a target-specific, context-rich, priority-ordered control bundle without compiling worker packets or granting content-generation authority.

escalate_to_double_when: owner requests audit of control selection
control selection changes high-risk claim inputs
subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Control-material selection is a controller/script-like routing step and is primarily proven by deterministic validation.

stages.completion_gate: S09ARichControlBundle is layered, priority-ordered, freshness-checked, conflict-resolved, and ready for S09B without worker guessing
stages.activation_policy: activate before per-unit packet assembly

stages.purpose: Compile one bounded, authority-safe per-unit TaskPacket from an S09A selected control bundle.

escalate_to_double_when: packet compiler or authority-boundary code changes
subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Task-packet assembly is an authority-boundary compiler step and is primarily proven by deterministic validation.

stages.completion_gate: S09BTaskPacketAssembly emits a validate_packet-clean, single-writer, completion-forbidden, non-recursive packet with lossless material closure, read obligations, and S09A usage labels preserved
stages.activation_policy: activate when a worker needs a section/unit packet

stages.purpose: Produce packet-bounded, claim-safe candidate prose units from validated S09B task packets with traceable controls and mandatory verifier evidence.

subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Main-text production is paper-facing semantic output; independent verification is mandatory before controller acceptance.

stages.completion_gate: S10CandidateTextReturn validates candidate prose, packet compliance, material hydration, read receipts, trace ledgers, CandidateArtifactReturn, writer/verifier evidence, and authority boundary; controller owns acceptance and completion
stages.activation_policy: activate when writing execution is needed

stages.purpose: Produce contract-bound candidate figures, captions, tables, algorithms, formulas, render plans, and export bundles without changing proof role, evidence meaning, or claim boundaries.

subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Artifact production is often deterministic but paper-facing; independent QA is required when it becomes final/export-facing.

stages.completion_gate: S11FigureCaptionArtifactBundle validates S08 contract compliance, source-data trace, panel/caption claim traces, image integrity, visual polish policy/report, accessibility, export manifest, CandidateArtifactReturn, and authority boundary without final export or completion claims
stages.activation_policy: activate when figure/caption/formal artifacts are touched

capability_adapters.authority_boundary: S11 remains controller-owned; adapter is helper only

stages.purpose: Compile a structured integrated manuscript candidate package and run consistency/backflow audits without PDF export, final acceptance, or uncontrolled rewriting.

subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Integration can create cross-section contradictions, so independent consistency verification is mandatory.

stages.completion_gate: S12IntegrationConsistencyReport validates module inventory, assembly manifest, structured integrated candidate, trace index, audits, findings/backflow queue, validator report, CandidateArtifactReturn, and no PDF/final/untracked rewrite boundary
stages.activation_policy: activate when multiple modules are integrated

stages.purpose: Compile actionable adversarial loss signals over the structured S12 integrated candidate; do not rewrite, repair, review PDF as primary object, or claim completion.

subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Adversarial review itself produces loss signals that must be independently validated for actionability and routing.

stages.completion_gate: S13AdversarialReviewReport validates structured S12 review object inventory, review scope, reviewer-panel and desk-risk reports, actionable severity/evidence/location findings, S14 routing, CandidateArtifactReturn, verifier evidence, and no rewrite/PDF-primary-review/repair/completion boundary
stages.activation_policy: activate when candidate manuscript/module exists

stages.purpose: Normalize accepted review/loss findings into nearest-responsible, owner-gated, bounded repair plans without executing repair.

escalate_to_double_when: repair plan affects multiple downstream nodes
subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Backflow planning is controller-owned routing; single planner lane is enough unless severe or multi-node repairs are involved.

stages.completion_gate: S14BackflowRepairPlan validates that every accepted finding is normalized, routed to a nearest responsible concrete stage/material, assigned a bounded local repair task, stale/protected nodes are explicit, owner gates are preserved, and no repair/completion is claimed
stages.activation_policy: activate when review finding exists

stages.purpose: Execute S14-authorized scoped repairs and regenerate only affected downstream outputs, returning candidate evidence without graph commit or export readiness claims.

subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Repair execution changes accepted materials; independent verification is mandatory to prove the finding is resolved without regressions.

stages.completion_gate: S15RepairExecutionReport validates strict packet acknowledgement, local diff scope, unrelated-node preservation, stale downstream regeneration/revalidation, finding-resolution evidence, no-new-high-severity scan, CandidateArtifactReturn, verifier evidence, and candidate-only/no-completion boundary
stages.activation_policy: activate when repair task exists

stages.purpose: Export a closed manuscript candidate into human-readable artifacts and prove delivery hygiene without claiming submission/publication readiness.

escalate_to_double_when: dirty-worktree or build hygiene is ambiguous
subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Export/handoff is mostly deterministic hygiene; escalate when the output is owner-facing or boundary-sensitive.

stages.completion_gate: S16ExportHandoffPackage proves explicit delivery target, target binding, upstream closure, separated readiness states, successful human-readable export/build, rendered-surface and semantic text checks, manifest hashes, repository hygiene classification, handoff completeness, feedback routing, and owner-gated/no-submission-publication boundary; compiled PDF targets require content-bearing PDF/source-writeback evidence and cannot be template-only or blocked; compiled semantic surface evidence must include body citation anchors, rendered reference entries, visual/formal artifact coverage, internal-lexicon absence, unresolved-risk leakage absence, and semantic-failure attribution routes.
stages.activation_policy: activate when PDF/export, owner preview, handoff, or pre-submission hygiene evidence is requested

stages.purpose: Define permissions, route governance, and state controls before automation.

escalate_to_double_when: runtime governance registry or validator semantics change
subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Governance records are registry/validator-controlled and should default to deterministic validation rather than extra agents.

stages.completion_gate: governance constraints are recorded and cannot inject route metadata into paper-facing cognition
stages.activation_policy: activate before automation/permissions/state changes

stages.purpose: Manage presentation, patent, profile-specific derivative packages, or other post-paper outputs after paper stability or explicit owner request.

subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Derivative outputs are outside the main paper cognition path and should stay single-lane until an owner-facing derivative is active.

stages.completion_gate: only explicit owner request can activate derivative output; otherwise recorded not-applicable/deferred
stages.activation_policy: activate only after paper content stability or explicit derivative request
